column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en

BG 1.B#ॐ श्री परमात्मने नमः\nअथ प्रथमोऽध्यायः अर्जुन विषाद योगः#om sri paramatmane namaha\natha prathamo dhyayaha arjuna vishada yogaha#Salutations to the most auspicious divine in all. Henceforth, the first chapter named Arjuna Vishada Yoga#om sri paramatmane—the most auspicious divine in all

BG 1.1#धृतराष्ट्र उवाच |
धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः |
मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ||1||#dhṛitarāśhtra uvācha
dharma-kṣhetre kuru-kṣhetre samavetā yuyutsavaḥ
māmakāḥ pāṇḍavāśhchaiva kimakurvata sañjaya#Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjay, after gathering on the holy field of Kurukshetra, and desiring to fight, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do?#dhṛitarāśhtraḥ uvācha—Dhritarashtra said

BG 1.2#सञ्जय उवाच ।
दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा ।
आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ||2||#sañjaya uvācha
dṛiṣhṭvā tu pāṇḍavānīkaṁ vyūḍhaṁ duryodhanastadā
āchāryamupasaṅgamya rājā vachanamabravīt#Sanjay said: On observing the Pandava army standing in military formation, King Duryodhan approached his teacher Dronacharya, and said the following words.#sanjayaḥ uvācha—Sanjay said

BG 1.3#पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणाम् आचार्य महतीं चमूम् ।\nव्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ||3||#paśhyaitāṁ pāṇḍu-putrāṇām āchārya mahatīṁ chamūm\nvyūḍhāṁ drupada-putreṇa tava śhiṣhyeṇa dhīmatā#Duryodhan said: Respected teacher! Behold the mighty army of the sons of Pandu, so expertly arrayed for battle by your own gifted disciple, the son of Drupad.#paśhya—behold

BG 1.4#अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि\nयुयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथ: ||4||#atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhi\nyuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥ#Behold in their ranks are many powerful warriors, like Yuyudhan, Virat, and Drupad, wielding mighty bows and equal in military prowess to Bheem and Arjun.#atra—here
column11: mahā-rathaḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors
column34: mahā-rathāḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors#

column1:
BG 1.5#अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि
युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथ: ||4||धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितान: काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान् |
पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गव: ||5||युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् |
सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथा: ||6||#atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhi
yuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥ
dhṛiṣhṭaketuśhchekitānaḥ kāśhirājaśhcha vīryavān
purujit kuntibhojaśhcha śhaibyaśhcha nara-puṅgavaḥ
yudhāmanyuśhcha vikrānta uttamaujāśhcha vīryavān
saubhadro draupadeyāśhcha sarva eva mahā-rathāḥ#BG 1.4-6:#atra—here
column11: mahā-rathaḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors
column34: mahā-rathāḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors#

column1:
BG 1.6#अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि
युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथ: ||4||धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितान: काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान् |
पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गव: ||5||युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् |
सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथा: ||6||#atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhi
yuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥ
dhṛiṣhṭaketuśhchekitānaḥ kāśhirājaśhcha vīryavān
purujit kuntibhojaśhcha śhaibyaśhcha nara-puṅgavaḥ
yudhāmanyuśhcha vikrānta uttamaujāśhcha vīryavān
saubhadro draupadeyāśhcha sarva eva mahā-rathāḥ#BG 1.4-6:#atra—here
column11: mahā-rathaḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors
column34: mahā-rathāḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors#

column1: BG 1.7#अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम |\nनायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ||7||#asmākaṁ tu viśhiṣhṭā ye tānnibodha dwijottama\nnāyakā mama sainyasya sanjñārthaṁ tānbravīmi te#O best of Brahmins, hear too about the principal generals on our side, who are especially qualified to lead. These I now recount unto you.#asmākam—ours

BG 1.8#भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जय: |\nअश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च ||8||#bhavānbhīṣhmaśhcha karṇaśhcha kṛipaśhcha samitiñjayaḥ\naśhvatthāmā vikarṇaśhcha saumadattis tathaiva cha#There are personalities like yourself, Bheeshma, Karna, Kripa, Ashwatthama, Vikarn, and Bhurishrava, who are ever victorious in battle.#bhavān—yourself

BG 1.9#अन्ये च बहव: शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविता: |नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणा: सर्वे युद्धविशारदा: ||9||#anye cha bahavaḥ śhūrā madarthe tyaktajīvitāḥ\nnānā-śhastra-praharaṇāḥ sarve yuddha-viśhāradāḥ#Also, there are many other heroic warriors, who are prepared to lay down their lives for my sake. They are all skilled in the art of warfare, and equipped with various kinds of weapons.#anye—others
column6: tyakta-jīvitāḥ—prepared to lay down their lives
column7: nānā-śhastra-praharaṇāḥ—equipped with various kinds of weapons
column9: yuddha-viśhāradāḥ—skilled in the art of warfare#

column1: BG 1.10#अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् |पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ||10||#aparyāptaṁ tadasmākaṁ balaṁ bhīṣhmābhirakṣhitam\nparyāptaṁ tvidameteṣhāṁ balaṁ bhīmābhirakṣhitam#The strength of our army is unlimited and we are safely marshalled by Grandsire Bheeshma, while the strength of the Pandava army, carefully marshalled by Bheem, is limited.#aparyāptam—unlimited

BG 1.11#अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिता: |\nभीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्त: सर्व एव हि ||11||#ayaneṣhu cha sarveṣhu yathā-bhāgamavasthitāḥ\nbhīṣhmamevābhirakṣhantu bhavantaḥ sarva eva hi#Therefore, I call upon all the generals of the Kaurava army now to give full support to Grandsire Bheeshma, even as you defend your respective strategic points.#ayaneṣhu—at the strategic points

BG 1.12#तस्य सञ्जनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्ध: पितामह: |\nसिंहनादं विनद्योच्चै: शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ||12||#tasya sañjanayan harṣhaṁ kuru-vṛiddhaḥ pitāmahaḥ\nsiṁha-nādaṁ vinadyochchaiḥ śhaṅkhaṁ dadhmau pratāpavān#Then, the grand old man of the Kuru dynasty, the glorious patriarch Bheeshma, roared like a lion, and blew his conch shell very loudly, giving joy to Duryodhan.#tasya—his
column4: kuru-vṛiddhaḥ—the grand old man of the Kuru dynasty (Bheeshma)

column1: BG 1.13#तत: शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखा: |\nसहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ||13||#tataḥ śhaṅkhāśhcha bheryaśhcha paṇavānaka-gomukhāḥ\nsahasaivābhyahanyanta sa śhabdastumulo ’bhavat#Thereafter, conches, kettledrums, bugles, trumpets, and horns suddenly blared forth, and their combined sound was overwhelming.#tataḥ—thereafter

BG 1.14#तत: श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ |\nमाधव: पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतु: ||14||#tataḥ śhvetairhayairyukte mahati syandane sthitau\nmādhavaḥ pāṇḍavaśhchaiva divyau śhaṅkhau pradadhmatuḥ#Then, from amidst the Pandava army, seated in a glorious chariot drawn by white horses, Madhav and Arjun blew their Divine conch shells.#tataḥ—then
column8: mādhavaḥ—Shree Krishna, the husband of the goddess of fortune, Lakshmi

column1: BG 1.15#पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जय: |\nपौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदर: ||15||#pāñchajanyaṁ hṛiṣhīkeśho devadattaṁ dhanañjayaḥ\npauṇḍraṁ dadhmau mahā-śhaṅkhaṁ bhīma-karmā vṛikodaraḥ#Hrishikesh blew his conch shell, called Panchajanya, and Arjun blew the Devadutta. Bheem, the voracious eater and performer of herculean tasks, blew his mighty conch, called Paundra.#pāñchajanyam—the conch shell named Panchajanya
column2: hṛiṣhīka-īśhaḥ—Shree Krishna, the Lord of the mind and senses

column1:
BG 1.16#अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिर: |
नकुल: सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ||16||
काश्यश्च परमेष्वास: शिखण्डी च महारथ: |
धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजित: ||17||
द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वश: पृथिवीपते |
सौभद्रश्च महाबाहु: शङ्खान्दध्मु: पृथक् पृथक् ||18||#anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣhṭhiraḥ
nakulaḥ sahadevaśhcha sughoṣha-maṇipuṣhpakau
kāśhyaśhcha parameṣhvāsaḥ śhikhaṇḍī cha mahā-rathaḥ
dhṛiṣhṭadyumno virāṭaśhcha sātyakiśh chāparājitaḥ
drupado draupadeyāśhcha sarvaśhaḥ pṛithivī-pate
saubhadraśhcha mahā-bāhuḥ śhaṅkhāndadhmuḥ pṛithak pṛithak#BG 1.16-18:#ananta-vijayam—the conch named Anantavijay
column8: sughoṣha-maṇipuṣhpakau—the conche shells named Sughosh and Manipushpak
column14: mahā-rathaḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors

column1:

काश्यश्च परमेष्वास: शिखण्डी च महारथ: |

द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वश: पृथिवीपते |
सौभद्रश्च महाबाहु: शङ्खान्दध्मु: पृथक् पृथक् ||18||#anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣhṭhiraḥ

dhṛiṣhṭadyumno virāṭaśhcha sātyakiśh chāparājitaḥ
drupado draupadeyāśhcha sarvaśhaḥ pṛithivī-pate
saubhadraśhcha mahā-bāhuḥ śhaṅkhāndadhmuḥ pṛithak pṛithak#BG 1.16-18:#ananta-vijayam—the conch named Anantavijay

BG 1.18#अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिर: |
नकुल: सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ||16||
काश्यश्च परमेष्वास: शिखण्डी च महारथ: |

सौभद्रश्च महाबाहु: शङ्खान्दध्मु: पृथक् पृथक् ||18||#anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣhṭhiraḥ

kāśhyaśhcha parameṣhvāsaḥ śhikhaṇḍī cha mahā-rathaḥ

column1: BG 1.19#स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् |नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलोऽभ्यनुनादयन् ||19||#sa ghoṣho dhārtarāṣhṭrāṇāṁ hṛidayāni vyadārayat\nnabhaśhcha pṛithivīṁ chaiva tumulo abhyanunādayan#The terrific sound thundered across the sky and the earth, and shattered the hearts of your sons, O Dhritarasthra.#saḥ—that

BG 1.20#अथ व्यवस्थितान्दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान् कपिध्वज: |
प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसम्पाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डव: ||20||
हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते |#atha vyavasthitān dṛiṣhṭvā dhārtarāṣhṭrān kapi-dhwajaḥ
pravṛitte śhastra-sampāte dhanurudyamya pāṇḍavaḥ
hṛiṣhīkeśhaṁ tadā vākyam idam āha mahī-pate#At that time, the son of Pandu, Arjun, who had the insignia of Hanuman on the flag of his chariot, took up his bow. Seeing your sons arrayed against him, O King, Arjun then spoke the following words to Shree Krishna.#atha—thereupon

BG 1.21#अर्जुन उवाच |
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ||21||
यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् |
कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे ||22||#arjuna uvācha
senayor ubhayor madhye rathaṁ sthāpaya me ’chyuta
yāvadetān nirīkṣhe ’haṁ yoddhu-kāmān avasthitān
kairmayā saha yoddhavyam asmin raṇa-samudyame#BG 1.21-22:#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 1.22#अर्जुन उवाच |
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ||21||
यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् |
कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे ||22||#arjuna uvācha
senayor ubhayor madhye rathaṁ sthāpaya me ’chyuta
yāvadetān nirīkṣhe ’haṁ yoddhu-kāmān avasthitān
kairmayā saha yoddhavyam asmin raṇa-samudyame#BG 1.21-22:#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 1.23#योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागता: |\nधार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षव: ||23||#yotsyamānān avekṣhe ’haṁ ya ete ’tra samāgatāḥ\ndhārtarāṣhṭrasya durbuddher yuddhe priya-chikīrṣhavaḥ#I desire to see those who have come here to fight on the side of the evil-minded son of Dhritarasthra, wishing to please him.#yotsyamānān—those who have come to fight

BG 1.24#सञ्जय उवाच |
एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत |
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ||24||#sañjaya uvācha
evam ukto hṛiṣhīkeśho guḍākeśhena bhārata
senayor ubhayor madhye sthāpayitvā rathottamam#Sanjay said: O Dhritarasthra, having thus been addressed by Arjun, the conqueror of sleep, Shree Krishna then drew the magnificent chariot between the two armies.#sañjayaḥ uvācha—Sanjay said
column4: hṛiṣhīkeśhaḥ—Shree Krishna, the Lord of the senses
column5: guḍākeśhena—by Arjun, the conqueror of sleep

column1: BG 1.25#भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखत: सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् |\nउवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति ||25||#bhīṣhma-droṇa-pramukhataḥ sarveṣhāṁ cha mahī-kṣhitām\nuvācha pārtha paśhyaitān samavetān kurūn iti#In the presence of Bheeshma, Dronacharya, and all the other kings, Shree Krishna said: O Parth, behold these Kurus gathered here.#bhīṣhma—Grandsire Bheeshma

BG 1.26#तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान् पार्थ: पितृ नथ पितामहान् |
आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातृ न्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा ||26||
श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि |#tatrāpaśhyat sthitān pārthaḥ pitṝīn atha pitāmahān
āchāryān mātulān bhrātṝīn putrān pautrān sakhīṁs tathā
śhvaśhurān suhṛidaśh chaiva senayor ubhayor api#There, Arjun could see stationed in both armies, his fathers, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, cousins, sons, nephews, grand-nephews, friends, fathers-in-law, and well-wishers.#tatra—there

BG 1.27#तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेय: सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ||27||\nकृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत् |#tān samīkṣhya sa kaunteyaḥ sarvān bandhūn avasthitān\nkṛipayā parayāviṣhṭo viṣhīdann idam abravīt#Seeing all his relatives present there, Arjun, the son of Kunti, was overwhelmed with compassion, and with deep sorrow, spoke the following words.#tān—these

BG 1.28#अर्जुन उवाच |
दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ||28||
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति |#arjuna uvācha
dṛiṣhṭvemaṁ sva-janaṁ kṛiṣhṇa yuyutsuṁ samupasthitam
sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṁ cha pariśhuṣhyati#Arjun said: O Krishna, seeing my own kinsmen arrayed for battle here and intent on killing each other, my limbs are giving way and my mouth is drying up.#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 1.29#वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ||29||
गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चै व परिदह्यते |
न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मन: ||30||
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव |
न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ||31||#vepathuśh cha śharīre me roma-harṣhaśh cha jāyate
gāṇḍīvaṁ sraṁsate hastāt tvak chaiva paridahyate
na cha śhaknomy avasthātuṁ bhramatīva cha me manaḥ
nimittāni cha paśhyāmi viparītāni keśhava
na cha śhreyo ’nupaśhyāmi hatvā sva-janam āhave#BG 1.29-31:#vepathuḥ—shuddering
column5: roma-harṣhaḥ—standing of bodily hair on end
column27: keśhava—Shree Krishna, killer of the Keshi demon

BG 1.32#न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च |
किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ||32||
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगा: सुखानि च |
त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ||33||#na kāṅkṣhe vijayaṁ kṛiṣhṇa na cha rājyaṁ sukhāni cha
kiṁ no rājyena govinda kiṁ bhogair jīvitena vā
yeṣhām arthe kāṅkṣhitaṁ no rājyaṁ bhogāḥ sukhāni cha
ta ime ’vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṁs tyaktvā dhanāni cha#BG 1.32-33:#na—nor
column13: govinda—Krishna, he who gives pleasure to the senses, he who is fond of cows

BG 1.34#आचार्या: पितर: पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहा: |
मातुला: श्वशुरा: पौत्रा: श्याला: सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ||34||
एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन |
अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतो: किं नु महीकृते ||35||#āchāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva cha pitāmahāḥ
mātulāḥ śhvaśhurāḥ pautrāḥ śhyālāḥ sambandhinas tathāetān na hantum ichchhāmi ghnato ’pi madhusūdana
api trailokya-rājyasya hetoḥ kiṁ nu mahī-kṛite#BG 1.34-35:#āchāryāḥ—teachers
column20: madhusūdana—Shree Krishna, killer of the demon Madhu

BG 1.36#निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्न: का प्रीति: स्याज्जनार्दन |
पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिन: ||36||
तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् |
स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिन: स्याम माधव ||37||#nihatya dhārtarāṣhṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdana
pāpam evāśhrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥ
tasmān nārhā vayaṁ hantuṁ dhārtarāṣhṭrān sa-bāndhavān
sva-janaṁ hi kathaṁ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava#BG 1.36-37:#nihatya—by killing
column7: janārdana—he who looks after the public, Shree Krishna
column28: mādhava—Shree Krishna, the husband of Yogmaya#

BG 1.38#यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतस: |
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ||38||
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभि: पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् |
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ||39||#yady apy ete na paśhyanti lobhopahata-chetasaḥ
kula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ mitra-drohe cha pātakam
kathaṁ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitum
kula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ prapaśhyadbhir janārdana#BG 1.38-39:#yadi api—even though
column24: janārdana—he who looks after the public, Shree Krishna#

kathaṁ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitum

column1: BG 1.40#कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्मा: सनातना: |\nधर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ||40||#kula-kṣhaye praṇaśhyanti kula-dharmāḥ sanātanāḥ\ndharme naṣhṭe kulaṁ kṛitsnam adharmo ’bhibhavaty uta#When a dynasty is destroyed, its traditions get vanquished, and the rest of the family becomes involved in irreligion.#kula-kṣhaye—in the destruction of a dynasty

BG 1.41#अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रिय: |\nस्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्कर: ||41||#adharmābhibhavāt kṛiṣhṇa praduṣhyanti kula-striyaḥ\nstrīṣhu duṣhṭāsu vārṣhṇeya jāyate varṇa-saṅkaraḥ#With the preponderance of vice, O Krishna, the women of the family become immoral
column2: and from the immorality of women, O descendent of Vrishni, unwanted progeny are born.#adharma—irreligion

column1: BG 1.42#सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च |\nपतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रिया: ||42||#saṅkaro narakāyaiva kula-ghnānāṁ kulasya cha\npatanti pitaro hy eṣhāṁ lupta-piṇḍodaka-kriyāḥ#An increase in unwanted children results in hellish life both for the family and for those who destroy the family. Deprived of sacrificial offerings, the ancestors of such corrupt families also fall.#saṅkaraḥ—unwanted children
column4: kula-ghnānām—for those who destroy the family

column1: BG 1.43#दोषैरेतै: कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकै: |\nउत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्मा: कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वता: ||43||#doṣhair etaiḥ kula-ghnānāṁ varṇa-saṅkara-kārakaiḥ\nutsādyante jāti-dharmāḥ kula-dharmāśh cha śhāśhvatāḥ#Through the evil deeds of those who destroy the family tradition and thus give rise to unwanted progeny, a variety of social and family welfare activities are ruined.#doṣhaiḥ—through evil deeds
column3: kula-ghnānām—of those who destroy the family

column1: BG 1.44#उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन |\nनरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ||44||#utsanna-kula-dharmāṇāṁ manuṣhyāṇāṁ janārdana\nnarake ‘niyataṁ vāso bhavatītyanuśhuśhruma#O Janardan (Krishna), I have heard from the learned that those who destroy family traditions dwell in hell for an indefinite period of time.#utsanna—destroyed
column4: janārdana—he who looks after the public, Shree Krishna
column10: anuśhuśhruma—I have heard from the learned#

column1:
BG 1.45#अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् |
यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यता: ||45||
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणय: |
धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ||46||#aho bata mahat pāpaṁ kartuṁ vyavasitā vayam
yad rājya-sukha-lobhena hantuṁ sva-janam udyatāḥ
yadi mām apratīkāram aśhastraṁ śhastra-pāṇayaḥ
dhārtarāṣhṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣhemataraṁ bhavet#BG 1.45-46:#aho—alas
column9: rājya-sukha-lobhena—driven by the desire for kingly pleasure

column1:
BG 1.46#अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् |
यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यता: ||45||
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणय: |
धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ||46||#aho bata mahat pāpaṁ kartuṁ vyavasitā vayam

yadi mām apratīkāram aśhastraṁ śhastra-pāṇayaḥ

column9: rājya-sukha-lobhena—driven by the desire for kingly pleasure

column1:
BG 1.47#सञ्जय उवाच |
एवमुक्त्वार्जुन: सङ्ख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् |
विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानस: ||47||#sañjaya uvācha
evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśhat
visṛijya sa-śharaṁ chāpaṁ śhoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ#Sanjay said: Speaking thus, Arjun cast aside his bow and arrows, and sank into the seat of his chariot, his mind in distress and overwhelmed with grief.#sañjayaḥ uvācha—Sanjay said

BG 1.E#ॐ तत् सत् इति श्रीमत् भगवत् गीतासु उपनिषत्सु
ब्रह्म-विद्यायां योग-शास्त्रे श्रीकृष्ण-अर्जुन-संवादे
अर्जुन-विषाद योगो नाम प्रथमोऽध्यायः#Om tat sat iti
Srimad Bhagavad Gitasu Upanishatsu
Brahma Vidyayam yogashastre Sri-Krishna-arujuna samvade
Arjuna Vishada Yogo Nama prathamodhyaha#Om Truth Is. Thus in the Upanishads of the glorious Bhagavad Gita, the science of the Eternal, the scripture of Yoga, the dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna ends the First discourse entitled The Yoga of Arjuna's Grief.#Om—Om
column11: Sri Krishna arujuna samvade—a dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna
column12: Arjuna Vishada Yogo Nama—Yoga of Arjuna's Grief
